data.title: Uzbekistan, U.S. company Air Products agree to accelerate construction of gas chemical complex in Bukhara
data.description:
Uzbekistan and one of the world's leading industrial gas production companies, Air Products (United States), plan to accelerate the implementation of a project to build a gas chemical complex (GCC) in Bukhara, as per Interfax.Uzbek President Shavkat Mirziyoyev held a meeting with Air Products Chief Executive Officer Eduardo Menezes as part of the business program of his working visit to New York, the president's press service said on Tuesday."The parties agreed to accelerate the implementation of new projects, including the construction of a gas chemical complex in Bukhara, the development of coal gasification technologies, and the production of environmentally friendly aviation fuel," the press service said.The meeting considered practical aspects of further expanding mutually beneficial cooperation. Air Products' total investment in the Uzbek economy exceeds $1 billion, the press service said. Among the implemented projects are an industrial gas processing unit at the GTL plant, hydrogen production at the Fergana Oil Refinery, the production of liquid carbon dioxide at the Navoiazot enterprise and the creation of a shock freezing center.Following the meeting, an agreement was reached to adopt a roadmap for advancing joint projects.Air Products specializes in producing and supplying industrial gases. Its market capitalization exceeds $63 billion. According to the company, it annually produces around 200 million tonnes of industrial gases in more than 50 countries and manages a network of around 750 production facilities.Uzbekistan signed an agreement with Air Products on the industrial processing of gases as part of the GCC project in May 2023.As reported, the project to build a gas chemical complex in the Bukhara region using methanol-to-olefins technology is being implemented with the participation of companies such as John Wood Group Plc (UK), the U.S. companies Air Products, Koch Industries Inc. and Chemtex Global Corp., Topsoe (Denmark), Versalis (Italy) and Sinopec (China).The complex, which will become the largest producer of polymer products in Central Asia, will have a capacity of over 1.11 million tonnes of products per year. The cost of the project was estimated at $5 billion.

mrchub.com

data.title: Petershill Partners Shares Jump on Plan to Return $921 Million in Cash Before Delisting
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

Petershill Partners' shares soared after the company proposed to cancel its listing on London's stock exchange and return $921 million of capital to free float shareholders.

The investment firm--which is operated and majority-owned by Goldman Sachs Asset Management--on Thursday said that shareholders will get 3.09 pounds, equivalent to $4.15, in cash for each of their shares. They will then be cancelled as Petershill exits the London bourse.

The decision comes after the board evaluated the company's strategic options. "The company's share price and valuation has, in the view of the board, not appropriately reflected the quality and underlying value of the company's assets, its strong financial performance and attractive growth prospects," it said.

The board concluded it should allow free float shareholders--who represent around a fifth of Petershill's capital--to realize their investment in cash at a level that reflects the group's attributes, it added. Private funds won't participate in the capital return--which will be funded by cash, deferred sale proceeds and fresh debt--and will remain invested in the delisted entity.

Petershill shares, which had closed at 2.31 pounds on Wednesday, rose 34% in early trading on Thursday to match the cash-out premium.

A meeting will be held on Nov. 3 for shareholders to approve the proposal, it said. Shareholders will be able to choose whether to receive the cash payout in U.S. dollars or in pound sterling at the average market exchange rate.

Shareholders are also entitled to a $0.052 interim dividend payable on Oct. 3, it added.

The total payment including this dividend values Petershill at around 3.4 billion pounds, a 35% premium on Wednesday's closing price, and reflecting a 16% return to shareholders who participated in its initial public offering, it said.

Separately, Petershill Partners backed its 2025 guidance and said that its partner-firms raised $19 billion of gross fee-eligible assets in the first half of the year.

Total income rose to $188.1 million from $146.3 million while the value of its financial assets and liabilities increased to $185.2 million from $72.3 million, it said. Pretax profit for the six months ended June 30 came in at $287.4 million, up from $163.9 million in the comparable prior-year period.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: TotalEnergies slips on reduced buybacks perspective
data.description:
** Shares in TotalEnergies slip 1%, to their lowest since early September, after French oil and gas major said it would consider scaling back its share buyback programme next year

** Berenberg, which downgraded the company to "hold" from "buy" says the reduction is aimed at maintaining low leverage level amid unfavourable oil price assumptions

** "The higher capex required leaves a higher reinvestment ratio than peers and less cash available for shareholder returns," Berenberg adds

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were up 0.2%​ YTD

data.title: Solar Energy Corp accepts bids for bond issue, bankers say
data.description:
India's Solar Energy Corp accepts bids worth 6 billion Indian rupees ($67.67 million), which for sale of bonds maturing in 10 years, three bankers said on Thursday.

The company will pay an annual coupon of 7.14% and had invited coupon and commitment bids for the issue on Thursday, they said.

Solar Energy did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: DAX Edges Lower
data.description:
The DAX in Frankfurt fell 0.5% to near 23,500 on Thursday, as traders weighed the prospect of new US tariffs and awaited key economic data along with comments from Federal Reserve officials.

In domestic data, Germany’s GfK consumer climate index improved slightly in October, surprising to the upside, though remaining in negative territory.

Sector-wise, healthcare stocks, construction and materials and industrials were the worst performers.

data.title: OptiCept Receives New Order from Juice Producer in Costa Rica
data.description:
OptiCept Technologies AB (publ) announces that the company today has received an order for its patented CEPT® system for pineapple juice extraction from an established producer in Costa Rica. Installation is planned for the fourth quarter of 2025.

The deal concerns an OPTICEPT® LJ7, a PEF system for liquid foods. This is the company’s second sale to the juice segment. The first sale announced was to a leading juice producer in Southeast Asia. Increased juice extraction, improved quality, and a more sustainable production chain are factors that make the technology particularly attractive in growing markets within the premium segment.

“We continue to introduce our solutions in key markets systematically, and a major reference customer in Costa Rica is strategically important since the country is one of the world’s largest exporters of pineapple juice. The deal validates the value we deliver, strengthens our position, and paves the way for more business in the region,” says Thomas Lundqvist, CEO of OptiCept Technologies.

Costa Rica is one of the world’s largest producers of pineapple products alongside the Philippines. With today’s deal, OptiCept now has reference customers in two of the most important markets for pineapple juice production globally. The global pineapple juice market was valued at approximately USD 3.89 billion in 2024 and is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.64% in the coming years.

For more information on the market potential for PEF (Pulsed Electric Field) in pineapple and OptiCept’s growth opportunities, see the company’s latest market deep dive via the link: OPTICEPT INSIGHTS

data.title: UK's Petershill Partners hits four-year high amid delisting plan
data.description:
** Shares of Petershill Partners up 33.6% at four-year high of 308.5p

** Stock top gainer on FTSE mid-cap index , which is down 0.36 %

** Board seeks cancellation of listing and trading on LSE; delisting expected on Dec 5

** Co to return $921 mln of capital to shareholders

** Freefloat shareholders to get $4.15 per share in cash and an interim dividend of $0.052 per share

** Total payout values entire issued ordinary share capital at about $4.5 bln

** Including session’s gains, stock up nearly 31% YTD

data.title: Colruyt falls after it reports drop in market share
data.description:
** Shares in Colruyt are down 4.2%, hitting their lowest price since May, after the Belgian supermarket chain reported a decline in its market share

** The company posted a 35-basis-point drop in market share in the first 5 months of the year, highlighting intensified competition and ongoing challenges

** Analysts at KBC Securities note that Colruyt's decision to open its Okay stores on Sundays from 2026 aims to defend market share but will likely result in higher costs due to spreading stable volumes across more days

** The broker adds that the removal of mandatory weekly closing days amplifies competitive pressures, with some Belgian stores already operating seven days a week

** While welcoming Colruyt's reiterated outlook for stable EBIT and net profit in FY25/26, KBC Securities believes the weighting of results towards H2 underscores the tough market environment

data.title: Swiss Central Bank on Hold as Tariffs Threaten Slowdown
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

Switzerland's central bank left its key interest rate at zero Thursday, ending a sequence of six straight cuts, despite the threat of slower growth as the economy adjusts to one of the highest tariffs set by President Trump.

Investors had mostly expected the decision after comments from officials at the central bank that signaled a reluctance to push the key rate back below zero, where it settled for almost eight years until September 2022.

The Swiss National Bank indicated that a further cut in the key rate is possible if a period of falling prices, or deflation, threatens.

"The SNB will continue to monitor the situation and adjust its monetary policy if necessary, in order to ensure price stability," it said.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds, swaps, call at 1:00 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was 0.2% down at 81,504 and the broader NSE Nifty 50 was 0.3% down at 24,990, as concerns over foreign outflows and U.S. visa curbs kept investors cautious.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee was nearly unchanged versus the U.S. dollar to 88.67, amid activity from importers and exporters as the quarter draws to a close.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 98.87 rupees, with the yield largely steady at 6.4883% as market participants await debt supply followed by the borrowing calendar for the fiscal second half.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was little changed at 5.46%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was nearly flat as well at 5.72%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.30%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.52%

data.title: Dalian iron ore rises on pre-holiday restocking demand
data.description:
By Lucas Liew

Prices of Dalian iron ore futures edged higher on Thursday, supported by pre-holiday restocking activity.

The most-traded January iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) was 0.25% higher at 805.5 yuan ($113.04) a metric ton.

The benchmark September iron ore (SZZFU5) on the Singapore Exchange was 0.09% lower at $105.6 a ton, as of 0711 GMT.

The market is closely watching the fallout from a contract dispute between China and leading miner BHP after state trading agency China Mineral Resources Group advised steel mills to halt purchases of BHP's Jinblebar blend fines - a key iron ore product commonly used in Chinese sintering operations, said analysts from ANZ.

Typhoon Ragasa might have impacted demand at some construction sites in South China, though it may recover after the Chinese National Day holiday as weather conditions improve, said broker Galaxy Futures.

High levels of hot metal production, a gauge of iron ore demand, provide a window for pre-holiday restocking, offering support to ore prices, said Chinese broker Hexun Futures.

Still, end-user demand during the golden September-October period remains to be seen, with steel mill profits under pressure, Hexun said.

Global crude steel production in August rose 0.3% year-on-year to 145.3 million tons, according to World Steel Association data, while crude steel output from top producer and consumer China fell 0.7%.

Elsewhere, the European Union is seeking to eliminate or substantially reduce tariffs on steel and aluminum exports to the U.S., though the world's largest economy has not yet responded to the proposal.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE rose, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) up 0.98% and 2%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange mostly gained ground. Rebar rose 0.32%, hot-rolled coil climbed 0.24%, and stainless steel increased 0.19%, while wire rod (SWRcv1) dipped 0.37%.

($1 = 7.1257 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Hungary Current Account Surplus Widens Sharply
data.description:
Hungary’s current account surplus sharply widened to EUR 1.39 billion in the second quarter of 2025, from EUR 0.08 billion in the corresponding period of the previous year.

The improvement was mainly due to a higher services surplus, which rose to EUR 2.84 billion from EUR 2.37 billion a year earlier.

The goods account also recovered, posting a EUR 0.09 billion surplus compared with a EUR 0.54 billion deficit in the previous year.

At the same time, secondary income rebounded to a EUR 0.20 billion surplus from a EUR 0.32 billion deficit.

Meanwhile, the primary income deficit widened further to EUR 1.73 billion, from EUR 1.43 billion in the second quarter of 2024.

data.title: Air Canada, Imperial Oil, Skeena Resources
data.description:
Securities analysts revised their ratings and price targets on several Canadian companies including Air Canada , Imperial Oil and Skeena Resources on Thursday.

HIGHLIGHTS

  • * Air Canada : Canaccord Genuity cuts target price to C$25 from C$28

  • * Imperial Oil : RBC raises target price to C$115 from C$108

  • * Skeena Resources Ltd : CIBC raises target price to C$36 from C$26

Following is a summary of research actions on Canadian companies reported by Reuters on Thursday. Stock entries are in alphabetical order.

  • * Air Canada : Canaccord Genuity cuts target price to C$25 from C$28

  • * Aya Gold & Silver Inc : CIBC raises target price to C$23 from C$22

  • * Discovery Silver Corp : CIBC raises target price to C$7 from C$6

  • * Endeavour Silver Corp : CIBC raises target price to C$12 from C$10

  • * Imperial Oil : RBC raises target price to C$115 from C$108

  • * Skeena Resources Ltd : CIBC raises target price to C$36 from C$26

  • * Vizsla Silver Corp : CIBC raises target price to C$7.5 from C$7

data.title: Quadient plunges to 2-1/2-year low after profit warning
data.description:
** Quadient shares fall over 13% to 13.8 euros ($16.21), their lowest price since January 2023, after the French software firm issued a profit warning for the current year

** For FY2025, Quadient now expects organic revenue falling by low single digit vs previous forecast acceleration

** EBIT now expected declining by low single digit vs previous forecast stable

** Midcap Partners analyst Jean-Pierre Tabart says the overall results are in line, but adds newsflow remains negative

** Portzamparc's Maxence Dhoury says the warning is already taken into account

** "While momentum remains sluggish, we still believe the valuation is too low to deter us from our buy recommendation," he says

($1 = 0.8514 euros)

data.title: Gold Edges Higher as Traders Await Key U.S. Data — Market Talk
data.description:
0739 GMT - Gold prices tick higher in early trade, supported by a weaker U.S. dollar as investors await key U.S. economic data for clues on the Federal Reserve's next policy move. Futures rise 0.1% to $3,773.70 a troy ounce, while the dollar index is down 0.1% to 97.80. "Prices slipped after new-home sales surged in August to their fastest pace since early 2022, boosting the dollar and raising concerns about fewer cuts ahead, while Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticised Fed Chair for lacking a clear easing agenda," MUFG says. "Still, gold remains near record highs, supported by last week's Fed rate cut, strong central bank demand, and record inflows into gold-backed ETFs." Traders are turning their attention to Thursday's weekly U.S. jobless claims data for insights into the labor market and Friday's PCE figures, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0732 GMT - China is likely to double down on national security and the high-tech sector in the next five years, UOB economist Ho Woei Chen says in a research note. Investors are closely watching the upcoming Fourth Plenum in October, which will review proposals for China's five-year development plan covering 2026 to 2030. China has benefited from strong investments in research and development and technological innovations, the economist notes. The country's growth target will be guided by its long-term target to double the size of the economy by 2035 from the 2020 level, the economist notes. A 4.5%-5.0% annual growth target for 2026-2030 would be necessary to meet that goal as China's growth will likely slow further in the 2030-2035 period, she adds. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0722 GMT - The Swiss National Bank is expected to leave interest rates on hold in a decision at 0730 GMT. This scenario should provide only slight support for the Swiss franc as the decision is now widely expected, Commerzbank analyst Michael Pfister says in a note. Although the SNB might reiterate its willingness to introduce negative interest rates if necessary, "it is likely to reserve such a step for an emergency," he says. The SNB is unlikely to opt to intervene more strongly in the foreign-exchange market by buying foreign currencies to weaken the franc, Pfister adds. The dollar trades steady at 0.7952 francs, while the euro rises 0.1% to 0.9342 francs. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)The dollar hit a two-week high on Wednesday. "U.S. Dollar Has Been Resilient to Recent Negative News — Market Talk," at 0649 GMT, incorrectly said in the first sentence that the two-week low was on Tuesday.

0649 GMT - The dollar edges slightly lower after hitting a two-week high on Tuesday. Barclays' currency strategists say the currency has failed to weaken meaningfully over the past couple of months despite "extraordinarily bearish events." The dollar went through shock moves when it weakened in February to May. Since then, it has traded in a relatively tight range, without substantial further weakness, despite weaker data and challeges to the Federal Reserve's credibility, they say in a note. Barclays expects that the U.S. economy will rebound in the months ahead, which could mean the U.S. currency remains resilient. The analysts are wary about threats to Fed independence, however. The DXY dollar index falls 0.1% to 97.798, having hit 97.921 on Wednesday. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0614 GMT - German Bunds are likely to remain stuck, with both the data calendar and the issuance pipeline thinning out, says Commerzbank Research's Erik Liem in a note. "Bunds remain in their tight range of just about 2 basis points, with eurozone PMIs, Ifo and [Federal Reserve Chair Jerome] Powell failing to provide a lasting impetus for outright yields," the rates strategist says. There is no government bond issuance in the eurozone on Thursday. The 10-year Bund yield trades flat at 2.749%, according to LSEG. (emese.bartha@wsj.oom)

0602 GMT - Long-dated U.S. Treasury yields edge lower in Asian trading hours ahead of a list of data, auction and Federal Reserve speakers. Durable goods, weekly jobless claims, third estimate GDP data for the second quarter and existing home sales are among the data that could potentially give input for the Treasury market. The U.S. Treasury will also auction $44 billion in seven-year notes, and several FOMC members are also due to speak throughout the day. The two-year Treasury yield rises 0.4 basis point to 3.601%, the 10-year yield declines 0.6 basis point to 4.140% and the 30-year yield falls 1.4 basis points to 4.743%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0543 GMT - Italian nominal government bonds, or BTPs, are expected to remain well supported in the coming months, but most of the spread tightening potential is behind us, says Luca Cazzulani, head of strategy at the Investment Institute by UniCredit in a note. "We expect the BTP-Bund spread to remain well supported into year-end given we see no imminent threat to appetite for carry," he says. The market environment has remained supportive of carry trades in September, with implied and realized volatility having continued to fall and excess liquidity remaining very abundant, he says. "This is an important factor pushing investors to search for carry," Cazzulani says. The 10-year BTP-Bund yield spread closed just below 85 basis points on Wednesday, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0524 GMT - The eurozone can expect a pickup in economic pace next year, analysts at Barclays write. Private-sector domestic demand remains subdued in the currency area for now, and growth in economic output looks likely to remain muted for the rest of this year, Barclays say. But next year, the eurozone can look forward to higher defense spending across the EU, as well as infrastructure stimulus in Germany and the gathering effects of lower interest rates, the bank says. "We expect momentum to pick up," the analysts say. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0457 GMT - Taiwan's 2025 economic growth will likely remain robust on strong capital expenditure in the chips and electronics sectors, ANZ economists say in a research note. The island's exports, capital expenditure and equity market will likely continue to benefit from demand for high-precision chips and data applications, they say. Taiwan's private consumption will likely hold up well because of the positive wealth effect thanks to the island's decent stock market performance, they say. Taiwan's robust economic growth and stable inflation should give its central bank enough room to keep interest rates stable through 2027, the economists say. The strong exports coupled with increased hedging activity among Taiwan's life insurance companies could lead to a stronger Taiwan dollar, they add. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0353 GMT - The Monetary Authority of Singapore is likely to keep its policy settings unchanged in October, ANZ's head of Asia research Khoon Goh writes in a report. Rising uncertainty from frequent changes to proposed U.S. tariffs has eased, Goh says. Singapore's economy has also performed much better than expected in 1H, while its outlook doesn't seem as dire as before, Goh says. The city-state's recent benign inflation indicates there isn't a reason for MAS to ease its policy further. "The MAS is forward-looking, and with inflation expected to rise towards its long-term average levels in 2026, there is no need to adjust policy," Goh adds. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0336 GMT - Thailand's August trade data suggest that the boost from front-loading ahead of U.S. tariffs is fading, Nomura economists write in a note. Goods export growth slowed to 5.8% on year in August from 11% in July, missing Nomura's expectations. There was a sharp decline in export growth to the U.S., they note. The U.S. tariffs on Thailand took effect early last month. However, Thailand's growth of exports to other countries also slowed, pointing to a broad-based slowdown in external demand, they say. Nomura maintains its 2025 GDP growth forecast for Thailand at 1.8%.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: European Stocks Fall on Thursday
data.description:
European stocks slipped into the red on Thursday, with both the STOXX 50 and STOXX 600 down 0.6%, following a subdued session the day before.

Market sentiment turned cautious as traders weighed the monetary policy outlook—particularly from the Federal Reserve—amid a series of scheduled speeches from officials today.

Economic data offered little support.

Germany’s GfK survey showed consumer confidence remains weak, while French figures also disappointed.

In contrast, EU passenger car registrations rose in August. Sector performance was mixed.

Healthcare led losses, with Siemens Healthineers plunging nearly 6% after US authorities launched new national security probes into imports of medical supplies, robotics, and industrial machinery.

On the upside, H&M surged about 9% after posting a stronger-than-expected Q3 profit.
